title: Sonerila trianae
taxonomy: Sonerila trianae is classified within the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the melastome family. This family consists of around 170 genera and approximately 5,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Sonerila is part of the tribe Sonerileae, which is one of the several tribes within the family Melastomataceae. Sonerila trianae is a species within this genus and tribe. The classification of Sonerila trianae is a result of ongoing botanical research and classification systems.

It has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Additionally, Sonerila trianae is said to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential in modern medicine.

intro: Sonerila trianae, also known as Sonerila, is a plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sonerila trianae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sonerila is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
